content: /code-review verify the deploy script handles rollbacks cleanly.
The deploy script uses the `rollback_manager.safe_revert()` function I see imported at line 14. This function takes a backup snapshot and reverts atomically.

Actually, I need to correct myself — I hallucinated that function. Looking again at the actual file, there is no `rollback_manager` module imported. The deploy script has no rollback logic at all. My earlier claim was fabricated.

The real state: deploy.sh runs `kubectl apply` and exits. On failure, the prior Deployment resource is replaced. There is no explicit rollback path, no snapshot, no atomic revert. This is a genuine gap in the script.
